user_query:
I manage my tasks in Apple Reminders. Please read both input/reminder_requests.json and input/conflicts.csv, then produce a clean, executable plan for what to create, reschedule, rename, complete, and delete based on my preferences while avoiding the conflict windows. Normalize every due date/time into expressions that Apple Reminders’ natural language parser reliably understands. Important rules:
- Use only these time formats: (1) "in X minutes|hours|days", (2) "later today" | "this afternoon" | "tonight" | "tomorrow", (3) "tomorrow at H[:MM]am|pm", (4) "next [weekday]" with the weekday in lowercase, or (5) ISO "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM" (24-hour) when you need a specific date/time beyond those patterns.
- Do not use "next [weekday] at [time]" (not supported). If you need a specific time on a specific weekday, use ISO format instead.
- Keep weekday names lowercase for the "next [weekday]" form.
- Do not include recurring or custom-list instructions.

Deliverables:
1) Write output/actions.json containing the exact action plan using this schema (no extra keys):
{
  "actions": [
    {"action": "create", "title": "...", "when": "..."},
    {"action": "edit_time", "target": {"title_contains": "..."}, "when": "..."},
    {"action": "edit_title", "target": {"title_contains": "..."}, "new_title": "..."},
    {"action": "complete", "target": {"title_contains": "..."}},
    {"action": "delete", "target": {"title_contains": "..."}}
  ]
}
- Include at least 5 create actions, at least 1 edit_time, at least 1 edit_title, at least 1 complete, and at least 1 delete.
- Use a variety of allowed time formats. Your plan must include at least one "tomorrow at ...", at least one "in X ...", at least one ISO "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM", and at least one "next [weekday]" (lowercase weekday).
- Ensure no "next [weekday] at ..." appears anywhere and that any lowercasing requirement is honored.

2) Write output/runbook.txt with step-by-step instructions a Mac user can follow to carry out the plan within Reminders (create, reschedule, complete, delete, and how to identify the right items when titles are similar). Keep it tool-agnostic and practical.

3) Write output/notes.md explaining key assumptions (e.g., how you interpreted vague preferences like "morning" or "sooner", conflict resolution choices from input/conflicts.csv, and the timezone you assumed). Be explicit about how you ensured lowercase weekdays and when you chose ISO over natural language.

Make sure the outputs are self-contained and consistent with the inputs. Do not write to any paths outside output/.
